D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Inattentional Blindness — Missing the Gorilla

Simons & Chabris (1999): when counting basketball passes, ~50% of observers fail to notice a person in a gorilla suit walking through the scene — attention gates conscious perception

Attention is a bottleneck — unattended features simply do not enter conscious report. Critically, colour of the unexpected object matters: a coloured gorilla against contrast (e.g. red on grey) is more noticeable than achromatic black-on-grey. Designers exploit and fight this all the time.

Slide unexpected-object salience to see detection-probability rise.
